# Disaggregated Inference Architectures

Disaggregation separates parts of the inference path so each can scale and
optimize differently.

## Chapter Job

Explain why systems separate prefill, decode, memory, routing, and serving
responsibilities.

## Patterns

- Prefill/decode disaggregation.
- KV cache movement or reuse.
- Router and scheduler separation.
- Multi-tier serving.
- CPU offload.
- Remote memory or cache services.
- Specialized hardware per phase.

## Tradeoffs

- Higher utilization versus higher coordination complexity.
- Better specialization versus more network dependence.
- Lower cost at scale versus harder debugging.
- Cleaner resource pools versus new failure modes.

## Placement

This chapter belongs before the specialized system survey because it gives the
reader a general vocabulary for architectures from SambaNova, Cerebras, Groq,
and related systems.
